Refers to the volume of aggressive asks executed 1 level or more away from the best offer, as a percentage of the total volume traded in that period.

Refers to the excess volume after the closing auction that stays on the book after the auction execution. This is the volume of bid orders at  the crossing price minus the volume of ask orders at the crossing price.

Refers to the excess notional volume after the closing auction that stays on the book after the auction execution. This is the notional volume of bid orders at the crossing price minus the notional volume of ask orders at the crossing price.
Closing auction order imbalance expressed as a percentage of the stock’s Average Daily Volume

Refers to the excess volume after the closing auction that stays on the book after the auction execution as a percentage of volume traded during the auction. This is the volume of bid orders at the crossing price minus the volume of ask orders at the crossing price.

Refers to the number of executions classified as bilateral addressable. This corresponds to the liquidity that is part of the price forming process, but requires a bilateral agreement between two parties to be accessible.

Refers to the notional value traded classified as bilateral addressable. This corresponds to the liquidity that is part of the price forming process, but requires a bilateral agreement between two parties to be accessible.

Refers to the number of shares trades classified as bilateral addressable. This corresponds to the liquidity that is part of the price forming process, but requires a bilateral agreement between two parties to be accessible.
Refers to the change (in basis points) between the mid-point price 1 minute prior to the end of continuous trading and the closing price of the auction on the regulated market.

Refers to the change (in basis points) between the last mid-point price during continuous trading and the closing price of the auction on the regulated market.
